title: Initial kpod build - DO NOT MERGE
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

First pass at getting kpod build in play.  The bats file tests will not run until Buildah can be installed into the Travis CI environment.  In the meantime I've created a shell script that can be run "by hand" to do the majority of the same testing.

This command will call buildah under the covers to build an image using a Dockerfile.

comments.body: :umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 3b72af6) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 3e04604) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 5c9694a) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Can we just check for the existance of /usr/bin/buildah, and skip the test if it does not exists for now?
@rhatdan That might work, then when @lsm5 gets back from pto I can see if I can push the requirement to get Buildah to install in Travis..

Assuming happy tests and appropriate +1's, I think this is now ready to go.  I decided to create a shell script to do some "by hand" testing with and I'm glad I did.  I found a number of issues that my initial testing did not uncover.
All Green.  Previous commit had some whitespace issues that gofmt didn't catch (grrrrrr).
I am waiting to get my podman rename in, before merging anything else. Since this would break that. Hopefully it should be merged soon, and then you will need to rebase.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 5770dc2) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Just posted a new patch need to test and will likely add another.  Feel free to hold the review until the next one.
